notes: Seeded by hand from real lessons in the free-context-hub project (Phase 11 closeout state).
20 queries across 4 groups: confident-hit, duplicate-trap, adversarial-miss, cross-topic.

Sprint 12.1a: duplicate-trap queries now list ALL known cluster members as targets — a hit is any cluster member, since dedup collapses clusters to one representative whose UUID varies. Without this, post-dedup recall would drop artificially when the representative differs from a fixed-UUID target.

Sprint 12.1e1 (2026-04-19): broadened 20→40 queries with 15 ambiguous-multi-target + 5 semantic-paraphrase entries. Mining from lesson_access_log yielded zero novel queries (all 20 distinct mined texts matched the goldenset itself from prior baseline runs) → zero-yield fallback per spec D4: extra synthesis instead of real-dogfood group. Each new query has a reasoning field (JSON-only, not in GoldenQuery TS type).

queries.query: how to handle pg UUID casing when building map lookups after RETURNING
why must undici userland version match the Node bundled version
pyenv-win python3.bat shim fails with multi-line -c bash arguments
npm test script lists test files explicitly so new ones silently skip
GET /api/lessons response shape uses items not lessons
should I invoke review-impl in post-review by default in the v2.2 workflow
NoProjectGuard hydration-safe approach for Next.js data pages
project CRUD POST PUT endpoints with validation rules
multi-project color and description schema additions
code review preference: review each file group separately after bulk implementation
max retry attempts must be 3 external API call guardrail
global search test retry pattern exponential backoff
valid impexp extra entry fixture
how to render a rainbow unicorn in WebGL with subsurface scattering
thermonuclear astrophysics of quark-gluon plasma at LHC
medieval falconry training manual hood design
integration test exponential backoff retry strategy
sprint 11.6c-sec DNS pinning body-stall timeout security polish
workflow gate state machine 12-phase workflow v2.2
agent context bootstrap end-to-end testing
how do we measure RAG ranking changes rigorously
queries.reasoning: Measurement-methodology cluster — 4 lessons cover delta-from-control, measure-first-baseline, A/B-archive-as-wiring-test, back-to-back-determinism. Any is a valid answer depending on which measurement aspect the reader cares about.

queries.query: how to build a near-duplicate key that doesn't over-collapse or under-collapse
queries.reasoning: Dedup-key-design cluster — normalizeForHash digit-collapse tradeoff, project_id+type inclusion, empty-content fallback, exact-id blindness. All 4 debate the same design question from different angles.

queries.query: invoke adversarial review in post-review by default
queries.reasoning: review-impl default-invocation cluster — one lesson justifies default-on, the other documents what /review-impl catches that POST-REVIEW misses. Both legitimately answer 'why default-invoke'.

queries.query: when salience boosts cause popular lessons to drown out specific hits
queries.reasoning: Popularity-feedback-loop diagnosis + mitigation — one lesson documents the loop (naive rank-weighted logging), the other documents the fix (query-conditional + composite relevance). Both are legitimate answers.

queries.query: why do baselines drift between runs
queries.reasoning: Baseline-determinism cluster — back-to-back-only determinism lesson, plus measure-first invariant that assumes within-session controls. Together they explain why drift appears across sessions but not within.

queries.query: handling numeric edge cases and avoiding silent failures
queries.reasoning: Numeric-handling cluster — NaN-through-clamp propagation + floating-point-tolerance-in-tests. Both address 'defensive numeric code' from different angles.

queries.query: integration test fixture timing and async work
queries.reasoning: Test-infra cluster — async-extraction defeats seed-and-verify, A/B archive as superior wiring test, back-to-back determinism. Three lessons about integration-test timing / non-determinism.

queries.query: noise floor for A/B diffs catches jitter
queries.reasoning: Noise-floor / variance-awareness cluster — diff-tool-must-consume-noise-floor, back-to-back-determinism defines the floor, delta-from-control builds on noise-floor, A/B archive as ground truth. All four are about variance-aware measurement.

queries.query: downstream consumers silently break when ranking shifts
queries.reasoning: Downstream-propagation cluster — retrieval-layer changes ripple into reflect tool, popularity feedback loop is a propagation phenomenon, measurement (delta-from-control) is how you catch propagation after the fact.

queries.query: how to avoid pool starvation when inserting asynchronously
queries.reasoning: Fire-and-forget / pool-contention cluster — one lesson on pool-sizing assumptions for write-paths, one on batching to avoid N+1 saturation. Both are mitigations for the same class of pool-starvation problem.

queries.query: how to keep project data isolated in queries and dedup
queries.reasoning: Multi-project isolation cluster — dedup-key-must-include-project_id, multi-project schema support (color+description), multi-project-aware salience batching. Three angles on 'project_id as a first-class key.'

queries.query: why use a composite signal not pure semantic for ranking boost
queries.reasoning: Composite-relevance cluster — query-conditional salience uses max(sem,fts), popularity-feedback lesson motivates why composite is needed. Different primary focus but both answer the 'why composite' question.

queries.query: empty content fields cause false dedup collisions
queries.reasoning: Dedup-failure-mode cluster (distinct from A2's 'how to build a key') — empty-content-fallback, project+type beyond hash, exact-id blindness. Three failure modes of under-specified dedup.

queries.query: does recency and frequency belong in retrieval ranking
queries.reasoning: Salience-as-bio-memory cluster — access-frequency needs conditioning, query-conditional composite signal. Both address the design question 'how should time/frequency factor into ranking'.

queries.query: why can't I write a simple seed-and-verify e2e test for dedup
queries.reasoning: Dedup-testing cluster — async-extraction + distillation defeat naive e2e, A/B baseline archive is the alternative wiring test. Reader asking 'how do I test dedup' gets either depending on whether they want a unit-ish e2e or a macro baseline.

queries.query: why does a Map miss entries right after an insert returns the key
queries.reasoning: Paraphrase of lesson-pg-uuid-casing (same target, 68b2f2cb) without using UUID/RETURNING/pg/canonical/casing keywords. Tests whether semantic retrieval finds the lesson from a symptom-described query.

queries.query: why does swapping http agents between the default and an installed one break streaming bodies
queries.reasoning: Paraphrase of lesson-undici-version-pinning (same target, 82e14086) without using undici/userland/bundled keywords. Tests semantic match on the abstract 'HTTP dispatcher interop' concept.

queries.query: defensive bounds via min and max don't stop bad numeric input from corrupting results
queries.reasoning: Paraphrase of the NaN-clamp lesson (05fde055) without using NaN/isFinite/clamp/propagates keywords. Tests semantic match on the abstract 'defensive math fails' concept.

queries.query: why is a brand new unit test file not executing when the suite passes
queries.reasoning: Paraphrase of lesson-npm-test-silent-skip (same target, 0f4be646) without using npm/explicitly/silently skipped keywords. Tests semantic match on the symptom rather than the cause.

queries.query: on Windows my inline Python script argument loses newlines at the batch-file boundary
queries.reasoning: Paraphrase of lesson-pyenv-python3-shim (same target, 8788dd3e) without using pyenv-win/python3.bat/shim keywords. Tests semantic match on the symptom.
